name: YC Seed Deck

name: Title Slide

guidance: layout_name: Q&A Slide. keywords: qa, questions, discussion, interactive. use_case: Invite audience questions. content_zones: Center: Q&A or Questions? text, Below: Contact info or submission method. visual_weight: Minimal text. cta_placement: None. recommended_for: End of presentations. avoid_for: Skip if no time. css_structure: display:flex; justify-content:center; align-items:center; font-size:64px. card_variant: none. metric_style: none. quote_style: none. grid_columns: 1. visual_treatment: centered. animation_class: animate-fade-up

details.content_zones: Center: Q&A or Questions? text, Below: Contact info or submission method

name: AIDA
guidance: formula_name: AIDA. keywords: aida, attention, interest, desire, action. components: Attention→Interest→Desire→Action. use_case: Lead-gen CTAs general persuasion. example_template: {Attention hook} → {Interesting detail} → {Desirable outcome} → {Action step}. emotion_trigger: Curiosity→Engagement→Want→Urgency. slide_type: CTA slides. source: Classic copywriting 1898

details.example_template: {Attention hook} → {Interesting detail} → {Desirable outcome} → {Action step}

name: PAS
guidance: formula_name: PAS. keywords: pas, problem, agitation, solution, dan kennedy. components: Problem→Agitate→Solution. use_case: Sales pages problem slides most reliable. example_template: You're struggling with {problem}. It's costing you {agitation}. {Solution} fixes this.. emotion_trigger: Frustration→Fear→Relief. slide_type: Problem slides. source: Dan Kennedy

details.keywords: pas, problem, agitation, solution, dan kennedy
details.use_case: Sales pages problem slides most reliable
details.example_template: You're struggling with {problem}. It's costing you {agitation}. {Solution} fixes this.

name: 4Ps
guidance: formula_name: 4Ps. keywords: 4ps, promise, picture, proof, push, ray edwards. components: Promise→Picture→Proof→Push. use_case: Home pages lead-gen. example_template: {Promise benefit} → {Picture future state} → {Proof it works} → {Push to act}. emotion_trigger: Hope→Vision→Trust→Action. slide_type: Solution slides. source: Ray Edwards

details.keywords: 4ps, promise, picture, proof, push, ray edwards
details.example_template: {Promise benefit} → {Picture future state} → {Proof it works} → {Push to act}

name: Before-After-Bridge
guidance: formula_name: Before-After-Bridge. keywords: bab, before, after, bridge, transformation. components: Before→After→Bridge. use_case: Transformation case studies. example_template: Before: {old state}. After: {new state}. Bridge: {how to get there}. emotion_trigger: Pain→Pleasure→Path. slide_type: Before/after slides. source: Copywriting classic

details.example_template: Before: {old state}. After: {new state}. Bridge: {how to get there}

name: QUEST
guidance: formula_name: QUEST. keywords: quest, qualify, understand, educate, stimulate, transition. components: Qualify→Understand→Educate→Stimulate→Transition. use_case: Matching solution to prospect. example_template: {Qualify audience} → {Show understanding} → {Educate on solution} → {Stimulate desire} → {Transition to CTA}. emotion_trigger: Recognition→Empathy→Learning→Excitement. slide_type: Educational slides. source: Michel Fortin

details.keywords: quest, qualify, understand, educate, stimulate, transition
details.example_template: {Qualify audience} → {Show understanding} → {Educate on solution} → {Stimulate desire} → {Transition to CTA}

name: Star-Story-Solution
guidance: formula_name: Star-Story-Solution. keywords: star, story, solution, narrative. components: Star→Story→Solution. use_case: Personality brands info products. example_template: {Introduce character} → {Tell their struggle} → {Reveal their solution}. emotion_trigger: Connection→Empathy→Hope. slide_type: Case study slides. source: CopyHackers

details.example_template: {Introduce character} → {Tell their struggle} → {Reveal their solution}

name: Feature-Advantage-Benefit
guidance: formula_name: Feature-Advantage-Benefit. keywords: fab, feature, advantage, benefit. components: Feature→Advantage→Benefit. use_case: Feature explanations product slides. example_template: {Feature name}: {What it does} → {Why that matters} → {How it helps you}. emotion_trigger: Curiosity→Understanding→Desire. slide_type: Feature slides. source: Sales training classic

details.example_template: {Feature name}: {What it does} → {Why that matters} → {How it helps you}

name: What If
guidance: formula_name: What If. keywords: what if, imagination, possibility, hook. components: What if + Possibility. use_case: Opening hooks vision slides. example_template: What if you could {desirable outcome} without {common obstacle}?. emotion_trigger: Wonder→Possibility. slide_type: Title problem slides. source: Headline formula

details.example_template: What if you could {desirable outcome} without {common obstacle}?

name: How To
guidance: formula_name: How To. keywords: how to, tutorial, guide, instruction. components: How to + Specific outcome. use_case: Educational actionable content. example_template: How to {achieve specific result} in {timeframe or steps}. emotion_trigger: Curiosity→Empowerment. slide_type: Educational slides. source: Headline formula

details.example_template: How to {achieve specific result} in {timeframe or steps}

name: Number List
guidance: formula_name: Number List. keywords: number, list, reasons, ways, tips. components: Number + Topic + Promise. use_case: Scannable benefit lists. example_template: {Number} {Ways/Reasons/Tips} to {achieve outcome}. emotion_trigger: Curiosity→Completeness. slide_type: Feature summary slides. source: Content marketing

details.example_template: {Number} {Ways/Reasons/Tips} to {achieve outcome}

name: Question Hook
guidance: formula_name: Question Hook. keywords: question, hook, curiosity, engagement. components: Question that implies answer. use_case: Opening engagement slides. example_template: {Question that reader answers yes to}? Here's how.. emotion_trigger: Recognition→Curiosity. slide_type: Opening slides. source: Rhetorical technique

details.example_template: {Question that reader answers yes to}? Here's how.

name: Proof Stack
guidance: formula_name: Proof Stack. keywords: proof, evidence, credibility, stats. components: Stat→Source→Implication. use_case: Building credibility trust. example_template: {Impressive stat} (Source: {credible source}). This means {implication for audience}.. emotion_trigger: Trust→Validation. slide_type: Traction proof slides. source: Social proof theory

details.example_template: {Impressive stat} (Source: {credible source}). This means {implication for audience}.

name: Future Pacing
guidance: formula_name: Future Pacing. keywords: future, vision, imagine, picture this. components: Imagine + Future state. use_case: Vision and aspiration slides. example_template: Imagine: {desirable future scenario}. That's what {solution} delivers.. emotion_trigger: Aspiration→Desire. slide_type: Solution CTA slides. source: NLP technique

details.example_template: Imagine: {desirable future scenario}. That's what {solution} delivers.

name: Social Proof
guidance: formula_name: Social Proof. keywords: social proof, testimonial, customers, trust. components: Who + Result + Quote. use_case: Credibility through others. example_template: {Customer name} increased {metric} by {amount}. '{Quote about experience}'. emotion_trigger: Trust→FOMO. slide_type: Testimonial slides. source: Robert Cialdini

details.example_template: {Customer name} increased {metric} by {amount}. '{Quote about experience}'

name: Scarcity Urgency
guidance: formula_name: Scarcity Urgency. keywords: scarcity, urgency, limited, deadline, fomo. components: Limited + Deadline + Consequence. use_case: Driving action urgency. example_template: Only {quantity} available. Offer ends {date}. {Consequence of missing out}.. emotion_trigger: Fear of loss→Action. slide_type: CTA closing slides. source: Cialdini influence

details.example_template: Only {quantity} available. Offer ends {date}. {Consequence of missing out}.

name: Cost of Inaction
guidance: formula_name: Cost of Inaction. keywords: cost, inaction, consequence, loss. components: Current cost + Future cost + Comparison. use_case: Motivating change. example_template: Every {timeframe} without {solution} costs you {quantified loss}. That's {larger number} per year.. emotion_trigger: Loss aversion→Urgency. slide_type: Problem agitation slides. source: Loss aversion psychology

details.example_template: Every {timeframe} without {solution} costs you {quantified loss}. That's {larger number} per year.

name: Simple Benefit
guidance: formula_name: Simple Benefit. keywords: benefit, value, outcome, result. components: You get + Specific benefit. use_case: Clear value communication. example_template: {Solution}: You get {specific tangible benefit}.. emotion_trigger: Clarity→Desire. slide_type: Any slide. source: Direct response

details.example_template: {Solution}: You get {specific tangible benefit}.

name: Objection Preempt
guidance: formula_name: Objection Preempt. keywords: objection, concern, but, however, faq. components: Objection + Response + Proof. use_case: Handling concerns proactively. example_template: You might think {objection}. Actually, {counter with proof}.. emotion_trigger: Doubt→Resolution. slide_type: FAQ objection slides. source: Sales training

details.example_template: You might think {objection}. Actually, {counter with proof}.

name: Comparison Frame
guidance: formula_name: Comparison Frame. keywords: comparison, versus, than, better, alternative. components: Us vs Them + Specific difference. use_case: Competitive positioning. example_template: {Competitor approach}: {limitation}. {Our approach}: {advantage}.. emotion_trigger: Evaluation→Preference. slide_type: Comparison slides. source: Positioning strategy

details.example_template: {Competitor approach}: {limitation}. {Our approach}: {advantage}.

name: Pain-Claim-Gain
guidance: formula_name: Pain-Claim-Gain. keywords: pcg, pain, claim, gain. components: Pain point→Bold claim→Specific gain. use_case: Concise value proposition. example_template: {Pain point}? {Bold claim about solution}. Result: {specific gain}.. emotion_trigger: Frustration→Hope→Excitement. slide_type: Problem/solution slides. source: Copywriting framework

details.example_template: {Pain point}? {Bold claim about solution}. Result: {specific gain}.

name: One Thing
guidance: formula_name: One Thing. keywords: one thing, single, focus, key. components: The one thing + Why it matters. use_case: Focus and clarity. example_template: The #1 thing {audience} needs to {outcome} is {one thing}.. emotion_trigger: Focus→Clarity. slide_type: Key message slides. source: Gary Keller concept

details.components: The one thing + Why it matters
details.example_template: The #1 thing {audience} needs to {outcome} is {one thing}.

name: Riddle Open
guidance: formula_name: Riddle Open. keywords: riddle, mystery, puzzle, question. components: Mystery + Reveal + Implication. use_case: Engagement through curiosity. example_template: {Intriguing mystery or paradox}. The answer: {reveal}. For you: {implication}.. emotion_trigger: Mystery→Insight. slide_type: Opening slides. source: Storytelling technique

details.example_template: {Intriguing mystery or paradox}. The answer: {reveal}. For you: {implication}.

name: Hero Journey
guidance: formula_name: Hero Journey. keywords: hero, journey, transformation, story. components: Ordinary→Call→Challenge→Triumph. use_case: Narrative structure. example_template: {Character in ordinary world} → {Discovers challenge} → {Overcomes with solution} → {Achieves transformation}. emotion_trigger: Identification→Tension→Triumph. slide_type: Full deck structure. source: Joseph Campbell

details.example_template: {Character in ordinary world} → {Discovers challenge} → {Overcomes with solution} → {Achieves transformation}

name: Value Stack
guidance: formula_name: Value Stack. keywords: value, stack, bundle, worth. components: Component + Value → Total value. use_case: Justifying price/investment. example_template: {Item 1} (Worth ${X}) + {Item 2} (Worth ${Y}) + ... = Total value ${Z}. Your investment: ${actual price}.. emotion_trigger: Value perception. slide_type: Pricing offer slides. source: Info product marketing

details.example_template: {Item 1} (Worth ${X}) + {Item 2} (Worth ${Y}) + ... = Total value ${Z}. Your investment: ${actual price}.

name: Power Statement
guidance: formula_name: Power Statement. keywords: power, statement, bold, declaration. components: Bold declaration + Supporting fact. use_case: Authority and confidence. example_template: {Bold declaration}. {Supporting evidence or fact}.. emotion_trigger: Confidence→Trust. slide_type: Key message slides. source: Thought leadership

details.example_template: {Bold declaration}. {Supporting evidence or fact}.

name: Bar Chart Vertical
guidance: chart_type: Bar Chart Vertical. keywords: bar, vertical, comparison, categories, ranking. best_for: Comparing values across categories. data_type: Categorical discrete. when_to_use: Comparing 3-12 categories, showing ranking, highlighting differences. when_to_avoid: Continuous time data trends. max_categories: 12. slide_context: Traction metrics feature comparison. css_implementation: Chart.js or CSS flexbox with height percentage bars. accessibility_notes: Always label axes include values. sources: Atlassian Data Charts

details.when_to_use: Comparing 3-12 categories, showing ranking, highlighting differences
details.css_implementation: Chart.js or CSS flexbox with height percentage bars

name: Bar Chart Horizontal
guidance: chart_type: Bar Chart Horizontal. keywords: horizontal bar, ranking, long labels, categories. best_for: Categories with long names ranking. data_type: Categorical discrete. when_to_use: Long category names, 5+ items, reading left-to-right natural. when_to_avoid: Few categories time series. max_categories: 15. slide_context: Team performance competitor analysis. css_implementation: CSS flexbox with width percentage bars. accessibility_notes: Natural reading direction for labels. sources: Datylon Blog

details.keywords: horizontal bar, ranking, long labels, categories
details.when_to_use: Long category names, 5+ items, reading left-to-right natural
details.css_implementation: CSS flexbox with width percentage bars

name: Line Chart
guidance: chart_type: Line Chart. keywords: line, trend, time series, growth, change over time. best_for: Showing trends over continuous time. data_type: Time series continuous. when_to_use: Time-based data, showing growth trajectory, 10+ data points. when_to_avoid: Categorical comparisons. max_categories: 50+ points. slide_context: Revenue growth MRR user growth. css_implementation: Chart.js line or SVG path element. accessibility_notes: Include data point markers for screen readers. sources: Tableau Best Practices

details.keywords: line, trend, time series, growth, change over time
details.when_to_use: Time-based data, showing growth trajectory, 10+ data points
details.css_implementation: Chart.js line or SVG path element
details.accessibility_notes: Include data point markers for screen readers

name: Area Chart
guidance: chart_type: Area Chart. keywords: area, cumulative, volume, trend, filled line. best_for: Showing volume or magnitude over time. data_type: Time series cumulative. when_to_use: Emphasizing magnitude, showing cumulative totals, comparing totals. when_to_avoid: Precise value comparison. max_categories: 3-4 series. slide_context: Revenue breakdown market share over time. css_implementation: Chart.js area or SVG path with fill. accessibility_notes: Use patterns not just colors for series. sources: Data Visualization Guide

details.keywords: area, cumulative, volume, trend, filled line
details.best_for: Showing volume or magnitude over time
details.when_to_use: Emphasizing magnitude, showing cumulative totals, comparing totals
details.slide_context: Revenue breakdown market share over time
details.css_implementation: Chart.js area or SVG path with fill
details.accessibility_notes: Use patterns not just colors for series

name: Pie Chart
guidance: chart_type: Pie Chart. keywords: pie, composition, percentage, parts, whole. best_for: Showing parts of a whole. data_type: Proportional percentage. when_to_use: 2-5 slices, showing simple composition, adds to 100%. when_to_avoid: More than 6 categories precise comparison. max_categories: 6 max. slide_context: Market share budget allocation simple splits. css_implementation: CSS conic-gradient or Chart.js pie. accessibility_notes: Never use 3D always include percentages. sources: FusionCharts Blog

details.when_to_use: 2-5 slices, showing simple composition, adds to 100%
details.slide_context: Market share budget allocation simple splits

name: Donut Chart
guidance: chart_type: Donut Chart. keywords: donut, composition, percentage, center metric. best_for: Parts of whole with center highlight. data_type: Proportional percentage. when_to_use: Like pie but need center space for key metric, 2-5 segments. when_to_avoid: Many categories. max_categories: 6 max. slide_context: Composition with key stat in center. css_implementation: CSS conic-gradient with inner circle. accessibility_notes: Same as pie include legend. sources: Modern alternative to pie

details.best_for: Parts of whole with center highlight
details.when_to_use: Like pie but need center space for key metric, 2-5 segments
details.slide_context: Composition with key stat in center

name: Stacked Bar
guidance: chart_type: Stacked Bar. keywords: stacked, composition, comparison, breakdown. best_for: Comparing composition across categories. data_type: Categorical + proportional. when_to_use: Showing composition AND comparison, segment contribution. when_to_avoid: Too many segments precise values. max_categories: 5 segments. slide_context: Revenue by segment across quarters. css_implementation: Chart.js stacked bar or CSS nested divs. accessibility_notes: Order segments consistently use legend. sources: Atlassian Data Charts

details.when_to_use: Showing composition AND comparison, segment contribution
details.css_implementation: Chart.js stacked bar or CSS nested divs

name: Grouped Bar
guidance: chart_type: Grouped Bar. keywords: grouped, clustered, side by side, multi-series. best_for: Comparing multiple metrics per category. data_type: Multi-series categorical. when_to_use: Direct comparison of 2-3 metrics per category. when_to_avoid: Too many groups (>4) or categories (>8). max_categories: 4 groups. slide_context: Feature comparison pricing tiers. css_implementation: Chart.js grouped bar CSS grid bars. accessibility_notes: Color code consistently across groups. sources: Data Visualization Best Practices

details.keywords: grouped, clustered, side by side, multi-series
details.when_to_use: Direct comparison of 2-3 metrics per category
details.css_implementation: Chart.js grouped bar CSS grid bars

name: 100% Stacked Bar
guidance: chart_type: 100% Stacked Bar. keywords: 100%, proportion, normalized, percentage. best_for: Comparing proportions across categories. data_type: Proportional comparative. when_to_use: Comparing percentage breakdown across categories, not absolute values. when_to_avoid: Showing absolute values. max_categories: 5 segments. slide_context: Market share comparison percentage breakdown. css_implementation: CSS flexbox 100% width segments. accessibility_notes: Clearly indicate percentage scale. sources: Proportional analysis

details.when_to_use: Comparing percentage breakdown across categories, not absolute values

name: Funnel Chart
guidance: chart_type: Funnel Chart. keywords: funnel, conversion, stages, drop-off, pipeline. best_for: Showing conversion or drop-off through stages. data_type: Sequential stage-based. when_to_use: Sales funnel, conversion rates, sequential process with decreasing values. when_to_avoid: Non-sequential data equal stages. max_categories: 6-8 stages. slide_context: User conversion sales pipeline. css_implementation: CSS trapezoid shapes or SVG. accessibility_notes: Label each stage with count and percentage. sources: Marketing/Sales standard

details.best_for: Showing conversion or drop-off through stages
details.when_to_use: Sales funnel, conversion rates, sequential process with decreasing values
details.accessibility_notes: Label each stage with count and percentage

name: Gauge Chart
